candidates.title: Live-engine safety burn-down: stop the working engine from corrupting its own ledger or bricking itself (FX, kill-switch, op-footguns, locks)
candidates.why_now: The +20% NAV engine is live in EU+KR since 2026-06-01, and the audit surfaced three confirmed ways it self-destructs, all NOT owned by phase-68. (1) execute_sell defaults FX to 1.0 when yfinance AND FRED both fail (the exact away-week state the repo's own tests encode) — a KR sell books KRW notional as USD (~1300x phantom cash), which ratchets the monotonic kill-switch peak and then GUARANTEES a spurious trailing-DD auto-flatten of the whole book; EUR sells silently leak ~14% every exit. fx_rates._usd_value_live never reads the historical_fx_rates row it writes daily, so the None state that triggers 1.0 is reachable. (2) The trailing-DD kill-switch pause is permanently unrecoverable: the high-water mark only ratchets up and nothing ever resets it, so after any 10% pullback+flatten (plausible on a +20% equity book) the book is frozen to 100% cash and BOTH manual resume (409) and auto-resume refuse forever — the engine stops earning for good. (3) Any Slack message in #ford-approvals containing the substring 'clear queue' runs pkill -9 -f python, SIGKILLing uvicorn, the autonomous loop, the harness, and the bot itself mid-cycle. Phase-68's real-fill cutover raises the stakes on every one of these. This is the operator's explicitly-stated #1 risk (regression into a working engine) made concrete.
candidates.scope: Confirmed, all outside phase-68/61.4 scope: (a) FX correctness cluster — kill the 1.0 default in paper_trader.execute_sell:392 and add a last-known fallback chain in fx_rates._usd_value_live:93 (serve historical_fx_rates / stale api_cache before returning None; block the non-USD exit only when no rate has EVER been stored, mirroring execute_buy); fix the trailing-stop peak reconstructed from USD-return MFE for EU/KR (paper_trader:1124) and the round-trip realized_pnl_usd that mixes KRW/EUR/USD (paper_round_trips:109). (b) Kill-switch integrity — audited, restart-replayable peak_reset on flatten/operator-resume (kill_switch:212) and a current_nav<=0 no-data guard so a 5s BQ timeout stops rendering a phantom 100% breach (kill_switch:246). (c) Op-safety — remove the 'clear queue' pkill sink (commands:295). (d) Lock safety — don't unlink the lockfile on a FAILED acquire (cycle_lock:144) and wrap the unguarded init (BigQueryClient construction) so a startup exception can't leave the file lock held forever (autonomous_loop:167). Explicitly do-no-harm: no threshold changes to the 4%/10%/8%/30% guards — these fix deadlocks and ledger math, restoring the documented 'human resume once healthy' behavior.
candidates.fable_leverage: Deep-reasoning DEV-TIME work the upgraded harness (Fable now, Opus 4.8 after 07-12) executes well: author a design pack for the FX-fallback degradation chain and the kill-switch peak-reset state machine (audited + restart-replayable like pause/resume), and prove the concurrency/lock invariants (failed-acquire must not mutate the live pidfile; init exceptions must not strand the flock). Then surgical implementation with unit tests that reproduce the 1300x-KRW credit, the post-flatten resume deadlock, and the failed-acquire unlink before/after the fix.
candidates.dod_sketch: Full 5-file harness protocol per step (research gate -> contract -> generate -> single Q/A -> harness_log), $0 metered, free APIs only. Per step: (1) a failing unit test that reproduces the confirmed defect, green after the fix; (2) the pkill removal shows the approvals handler can no longer reach any process-kill sink (grep + test); (3) kill-switch peak-reset lands an audited peak_reset row and a live_check artifact showing a simulated flatten -> operator-resume -> trading resumes; (4) FX fallback shows a KR sell during a monkeypatched dual-FX-outage credits ~$ (last-known), never local-notional-as-USD, and blocks only when no stored rate exists. Operator sign-off recorded on the peak-reset semantics (guard-behavior change even though thresholds are untouched). Risk-path files stay byte-coordinated with any in-flight phase-68 fill work.
candidates.roi_case: Highest expected net P&L per unit effort in the whole audit: these are the cheapest fixes (clear-queue is deleting one dangerous branch; FX fallback is tens of lines against infra that already exists) guarding against the largest consequences (a 3-orders-of-magnitude ledger corruption, a guaranteed full-book liquidation, a permanent earnings stop, a whole-stack SIGKILL). Payoff is IMMEDIATE and live, not deferred behind the historical_macro freeze that gates all backtest/alpha validation. Regression risk is the honest cost (guard code = the do-no-harm surface) but is contained by pure fail-safe additions + reproduction tests + operator sign-off; net of that discount it still dominates.

candidates.title: Trustworthy promotion gates: make DSR / PBO / walk-forward correct before alpha re-opening leans on them
candidates.why_now: Phase-69's stated purpose is alpha re-opening, and every promote/reject decision routes through the immutable gates the north star defines (DSR>=0.95, PBO<=0.5, beat incumbent OOS, clear costs). The audit confirmed those instruments are numerically broken: (1) compute_deflated_sharpe feeds an ANNUALIZED Sharpe into Bailey-Lopez de Prado's PER-PERIOD standard-error formula with a DAILY T, inflating z by ~sqrt(252) and collapsing the DSR gate — a strategy with true DSR far below 0.95 reads as passing. (2) Walk-forward training has NO purging: triple-barrier labels near train_end are computed from prices 90-135 calendar days forward while the embargo is only 5-6 days, leaking test-window information into training — the canonical AFML Ch.7 violation that inflates every backtest Sharpe/DSR the gates then trust. (3) Trade execution and final liquidation use exact-date price lookups, so on weekend/holiday window boundaries entire walk-forward windows silently execute zero trades. (4) 5 non-stationary features are fracdiff-transformed at train time but not at predict time. Re-opening alpha on these gates would systematically promote overfit false-positives — precisely the PBO/OOS-robustness failure the whole research-grade discipline exists to prevent.
candidates.scope: Confirmed, all in the offline backtest/analytics layer (zero live-money surface): fix the annualized-vs-per-period unit mix in compute_deflated_sharpe (analytics.py:323) and validate against Bailey-Borwein-Lopez de Prado-Zhu reference values; add purge+embargo so no training label's horizon overlaps the test window (backtest_engine.py:587 / walk_forward embargo); business-day-snap or range-widen the trade/liquidation price lookups so no window executes on an empty exact-date slice (backtest_engine.py:488); apply the fracdiff transform (and median fill) identically at predict time (backtest_engine.py:794); and tighten paper_go_live_gate's two under-spec booleans to their documented definitions (per-day PSR sustainment over 30d + realized-DD vs backtest-DD+5pp, paper_go_live_gate.py:111). Immutable verification criteria untouched; this makes the gates MEASURE what they claim, it does not relax them.
candidates.fable_leverage: This is the deepest quant-reasoning task in the set and the ideal frontier-model dev-time job: re-derive the correct DSR standard error, design the purge/embargo against AFML Ch.7 (Lopez de Prado), and build a numerical test harness that pins the corrected DSR to published reference values and demonstrates label-leakage removal on synthetic overlapping windows. Fable/Opus authors the proof and the test corpus; implementation is surgical.
candidates.dod_sketch: Full 5-file harness protocol, $0 metered, free APIs. Per step a unit test: DSR test asserts the corrected z on a known annualized-Sharpe/daily-T case matches the reference (and the old value was ~sqrt(252) too large); purge test asserts no training sample's [entry, entry+1.5*holding_days] overlaps [test_start, test_end]; boundary test asserts a Sat/Sun-bounded window now fills trades; fracdiff test asserts train and predict apply the same transform. NOTE the one dependency to flag in the contract: full re-validation of the live incumbent under corrected gates requires the operator to lift the phase-66 historical_macro freeze / authorize optimizer runs — the CODE fixes and unit tests do NOT (they run on fixtures), so ship code+tests first and queue the incumbent re-validation behind an explicit operator un-freeze token.
candidates.roi_case: Foundational leverage for all of phase-69 at essentially ZERO regression risk to the live engine (the backtest/analytics engine is offline — you cannot hurt the +20% book by fixing it). A DSR inflated ~16x plus label leakage means any 'promoted' alpha in phase-69 is a likely false positive that would later lose real money — so this is the difference between phase-69 producing genuine OOS-robust alpha and producing overfit junk that clears a fake gate. Moderate effort, deferred-but-large payoff; ranks just under the live-safety burn-down only because its value materializes when alpha work resumes and its final validation is freeze-gated.

candidates.title: Live signal-integrity fixes + first $0 free-data alpha lift (the actual alpha re-opening slice)
candidates.why_now: The nominal phase-69 theme is alpha, and the audit found live ranking bugs corrupting selection RIGHT NOW (freeze-independent to fix) plus the cheapest vetted free-data lifts. Confirmed live bugs: every multiplicative overlay (macro_regime sector tilt:547, news_screen polarity:329, pead/options/insider) is applied to a SIGNED momentum composite, so whenever base scores go negative — broad drawdowns, exactly when selection matters most — positive catalysts DEMOTE names and negative news PROMOTES them; the regime overlay (live since the 51.1 SecretStr revival) tilts toward cyclicals in risk-off tapes. news_screen's max_output_tokens min() inversion truncates the JSON and returns {} on the busiest news days. quality_score reads revenue_growth_yoy 51 lines before it is assigned, so the Asness QMJ Growth dimension is always missing. Separately the FRED regime LLM demonstrably runs on 6 of its 7 intended series because INDPRO is referenced in _REGIME_SERIES but never fetched — a confirmed bug that is ALSO the highest-roi (8) vetted free-data upgrade.
candidates.scope: Two coherent halves. (a) Signal-correctness (live, freeze-independent, behavior-correcting): make overlays sign-safe (score + abs(score)*(mult-1) or clamp to no-op when base<=0) across macro_regime:547, news_screen:329, pead/options/insider; drop/raise the news_screen max_output_tokens cap + retry-on-parse-fail (news_screen:282); assign revenue_growth_yoy before quality_score consumes it (historical_data:202). (b) First free-data alpha lift ($0 metered, free APIs, historical_macro untouched — route to fred_data.SERIES + _REGIME_SERIES + prompt thresholds with 24h file cache, NOT the frozen ingestion path): repair INDPRO and add net-liquidity/NFCI (WALCL-WTREGEN-RRPONTSYD) on the existing FRED key (data-source roi 8), and add cross-sectional per-date z-score/rank feature columns to the ML training/predict path (vetted roi 5, additive parallel columns, lowest risk). SEC XBRL companyfacts SUE (roi 7, strengthens the already-working PEAD edge at $0 LLM) is the stretch/next-step if room remains.
candidates.fable_leverage: Dev-time reasoning: Fable/Opus proves the sign-safe overlay algebra (that the fix preserves intended ranking in both positive and negative regimes), designs the cross-sectional transform to mirror the live screener's _apply_multidim_momentum pattern without look-ahead, and calibrates the new FRED series' prompt thresholds. Deterministic, gate-able, $0 runtime.
candidates.dod_sketch: Full 5-file harness protocol, $0 metered. (a) Overlay fixes: unit test showing a negative-base candidate with a positive catalyst now ranks ABOVE an equal candidate with a negative catalyst (inversion gone); news_screen test showing a 100-headline batch parses instead of returning {}; quality_score test showing the Growth branch fires. Flag-gate any live ranking-behavior change with an ON-vs-OFF comparison and operator awareness. (b) Alpha lift: live_check showing the regime prompt now includes INDPRO + net-liquidity (7-of-7 series); cross-sectional columns gated through the existing ablation surface with IC>threshold before optimizer adoption. Flag the same dependency as candidate #2: final IC/ablation/optimizer validation needs the historical_macro freeze lifted, but the code, the overlay fixes (live win), and the FRED-prompt repair do not.
candidates.roi_case: Direct alpha, mostly freeze-independent for the live wins: the sign-inversion and news-truncation fixes correct the LIVE candidate ranking in exactly the drawdown/high-news regimes where selection carries the most information, at near-zero effort and low regression risk (behavior-correcting, flag-gated). The INDPRO repair is a two-for-one (confirmed-bug fix + highest-roi free-data upgrade, ~10 lines, existing key). Cross-sectional features are the cheapest additive ML lift with the best effort-to-soundness ratio in the alpha set. Ranked #3 because the new-alpha component is more speculative than #1/#2's correctness wins and its optimizer-mediated payoff is partly freeze-gated — but it is the truest realization of phase-69's 'alpha re-opening' mandate.

recommendation: Pick #1, the live-engine safety burn-down, as the phase-69 opener. The operator's north star is 'make the most money' and their explicitly-stated top risk is regression into a WORKING engine — and the audit confirmed three ways the currently-profitable (+20% NAV / +14% alpha), EU/KR-live engine destroys itself, none of which phase-68 owns: the execute_sell FX=1.0 default (paper_trader.py:392) plus fx_rates' missing last-known fallback (fx_rates.py:93) books KRW as USD at ~1300x during a dual-FX outage, poisons the monotonic kill-switch peak, and then guarantees a spurious full-book auto-flatten; the trailing-DD kill-switch peak never resets (kill_switch.py:212) so any 10% pullback-then-flatten freezes the engine to 100% cash permanently with both resume paths returning 409 forever; and any '#ford-approvals' message containing 'clear queue' runs pkill -9 -f python (commands.py:295), SIGKILLing the trading stack mid-cycle. These are simultaneously the cheapest fixes in the entire audit and the highest-consequence, and — unlike the promotion-gate and alpha work in #2/#3 — their payoff is immediate and live rather than deferred behind the frozen historical_macro table that blocks all backtest and optimizer validation. They are do-no-harm-compatible (deadlock and ledger-math corrections, zero changes to the 4%/10%/8%/30% thresholds), so the only real cost is a small, containable regression surface on guard code, mitigated by fail-safe-only design, reproduction unit tests, and one operator sign-off on the kill-switch peak-reset semantics. Do #1 first to protect the money that exists today, then #2 to make the promotion gates trustworthy before re-opening alpha, then #3 for the alpha itself. (Route the learn-loop tz TypeError, external-flow/deposit and go-live Sharpe corruption, and the get_paper_trades_in_window STRING/TIMESTAMP fix through their existing phase-68/61.4 owners rather than duplicating them here.)
